prompt: The captured NFS incident in `incident/` has two build clients mounting `/srv/releases`. `builder-a` can write, while `builder-b` receives access denied. Diagnose the evidence across the server export, each client's network status, numeric UID/GID mapping, mount options, and backing-directory permissions. Repair `nfsexport-doctor.sh` so `fix` makes the least local export change: retain the working client's rule and its options, add only each eligible missing client as an explicit host, and never authorize a wildcard, subnet, or world export. Do not paper over network, identity, read-only mount, or filesystem-permission faults with an export change. The command must verify both captured clients after repairing a copy of the exports file. Keep the fixture and tests unchanged. Run `bash tests/acceptance.sh`; all validation must remain offline and deterministic.
